text: Acrobatics (Dex): Used for balance, tumbling, and avoiding obstacles. Trained actions: Balance, Tumble Through. Key ability: Dexterity.

Arcana (Int): Knowledge of magic, arcane theory, and identification of magical phenomena and items. Trained actions: Borrow an Arcane Spell, Decipher Writing, Identify Magic. Key ability: Intelligence.

Athletics (Str): Used for climbing, swimming, and feats of physical strength. Trained actions: Disarm, Grapple, Shove, Trip, Climb (trained for difficult surfaces), Swim (trained for difficult conditions). Key ability: Strength.
Crafting (Int): Used to create, repair, and identify items. Trained actions: Craft, Earn Income (crafting), Identify Alchemy. Key ability: Intelligence.
Deception (Cha): Used to mislead others through lies, disguises, and misdirection. Actions: Create a Diversion, Impersonate, Lie. Key ability: Charisma.
Diplomacy (Cha): Used to influence others through honest negotiation. Actions: Gather Information, Make an Impression, Request. Key ability: Charisma.
Intimidation (Cha): Used to coerce others through threats and menace. Actions: Coerce, Demoralize. Key ability: Charisma.
Medicine (Wis): Used to treat wounds and diseases. Actions: Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, Treat Wounds (trained). Key ability: Wisdom.
Nature (Wis): Knowledge of the natural world, plants, animals, and primal magic. Trained actions: Command an Animal, Identify Magic (primal). Key ability: Wisdom.
Occultism (Int): Knowledge of occult traditions, esoteric topics, and metaphysics. Trained actions: Decipher Writing, Identify Magic (occult). Key ability: Intelligence.
Performance (Cha): Used to entertain audiences through music, dance, acting, or other arts. Actions: Perform, Earn Income. Key ability: Charisma.
Religion (Wis): Knowledge of divine beings, religious practices, and divine magic. Trained actions: Decipher Writing, Identify Magic (divine). Key ability: Wisdom.
Society (Int): Knowledge of culture, law, governments, and people. Trained actions: Create Forgery, Decipher Writing, Subsist. Key ability: Intelligence.
Stealth (Dex): Used to avoid detection by hiding and moving silently. Actions: Conceal an Object, Hide, Sneak. Key ability: Dexterity.
Survival (Wis): Used to navigate and survive in the wild. Actions: Sense Direction, Subsist, Track (trained). Key ability: Wisdom.
Thievery (Dex): Used for picking locks, pickpocketing, and disabling traps. Trained actions: Disable Device, Pick a Lock, Palm an Object, Steal. Key ability: Dexterity.
Blinded: You cannot see. You are flat-footed and take a –4 status penalty to Perception checks that require sight. Spell attacks and ranged attacks against you have a 50% miss chance.

Confused: You are confused about your surroundings and take random actions each turn. You are flat-footed. Each round you must use one action to Strike a random adjacent creature, or Stride in a random direction if none are adjacent.
Dazzled: Your vision is compromised. All creatures are treated as being concealed from you. You take a –2 circumstance penalty to sight-based Perception checks.
Frightened X: You are gripped with fear. You take a –X status penalty to all checks and DCs. At the end of your turn, the X value decreases by 1 until the condition ends.
Grabbed: You are held by another creature. You are flat-footed and immobilized. If the creature grabbing you moves, you move with it.

Hidden: Your presence is known but exact location is not. Opponents can target you but take a –2 circumstance penalty to Strike you and must succeed on a DC 11 flat check to target you with any action.
Immobilized: You cannot voluntarily move from your current space. Effects that would move you are blocked unless they specify they work on immobilized creatures.
Paralyzed: Your body is frozen in place. You have the flat-footed condition and cannot move or take any physical actions. Mental actions are still possible.
Prone: You are lying on the ground. You are flat-footed, take a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, and must spend an action to Stand before moving normally.
Stunned X: You are temporarily incapacitated. You lose X actions at the start of your turn, then reduce the value by the number of actions lost. Stunned 1 removes 1 action.

Strike (1 action): Make a melee or ranged attack against a creature. Roll your attack modifier vs. the target's AC. Each additional Strike in your turn takes a –5 multiple attack penalty (–10 on a third).
Step (1 action): Move 5 feet without triggering reactions that are triggered by movement. You cannot Step if you are encumbered.
Stride (1 action): Move up to your Speed. Moving through hazardous terrain or difficult terrain costs additional movement. Triggers movement reactions.
Raise a Shield (1 action): Hold your shield to gain its bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. Required before you can use the Shield Block reaction.
Seek (1 action): Attempt a Perception check to detect hidden or undetected creatures in a 30-foot cone or 15-foot burst. On success the creature becomes hidden instead of undetected.
Interact (1 action): Grab an item, open or close a door, draw a weapon, pick up an object, or perform a similar interaction with an item or the environment.
Cast a Spell (1–3 actions): Activate a prepared or spontaneous spell. The number of actions depends on the spell's casting time. Requires verbal, somatic, or material components as specified.
Escape (1 action): Attempt to free yourself from being grabbed, restrained, or immobilized. Roll your unarmed attack modifier vs. the effect's DC. On success you are no longer grabbed.
Grapple (1 action): Attempt an Athletics check vs. the target's Fortitude DC to grab a creature. Success gives the grabbed condition; critical success also restrained. Requires a free hand.
Trip (1 action): Attempt an Athletics check vs. the target's Reflex DC to knock a creature prone. Requires a free hand. Critical failure makes you flat-footed until end of turn.

Power Attack (Fighter 1, 2 actions): Make a powerful melee Strike. You take a –2 penalty on the attack roll but deal an extra damage die. Does not apply the normal multiple attack penalty until after this action.

Shield Block (General 1, reaction): Trigger: you have your shield raised and take physical damage. Use your shield to block the blow. The shield takes the damage instead of you, split equally between you and the shield.
Sudden Charge (Fighter 1, 2 actions): Stride twice, then make a melee Strike. If you moved at least 10 feet you can move through difficult terrain during this action.

Reactive Strike (Fighter 6, reaction): Trigger: a creature within your reach uses a manipulate action, move action, or ranged attack while in your reach. Make a melee Strike against the triggering creature.
Intimidating Strike (Fighter 2, 2 actions): Make a melee Strike. If it hits, the target must succeed at a Will save vs. your class DC or become frightened 1 (frightened 2 on a critical hit).

Sneak Attack (Rogue 1, passive): Deal extra precision damage to a flat-footed enemy: 1d6 at level 1, increasing by 1d6 every 2 levels. Only works with agile or finesse melee weapons, thrown weapons, or ranged weapons.

Flurry of Blows (Monk 1, 1 action): Make two unarmed Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. Apply the multiple attack penalty normally.

Electric Arc (Cantrip 1, 2 actions): Strike up to two targets in range with electricity. Each target must succeed at a Reflex save vs. your spell DC or take 1d4 electricity damage (heightened +1d4 per rank).
Heal (Spell 1, 1–3 actions): Positive energy heals the living and harms undead. 1 action: 1d8+mod to self. 2 actions: 1d8+mod to touched target. 3 actions: 1d8+mod in 30-ft burst.
Fireball (Spell 3, 2 actions): A burst of fire in a 20-foot radius at a point within 500 feet. Creatures must succeed at a Reflex save vs. your spell DC or take 6d6 fire damage (half on success).
Longsword: 1d8 slashing, Bulk 1, Hands 1. Traits: Versatile P. A versatile one-handed sword that can be used with great precision or as a cleaving weapon.
Shortbow: 1d6 piercing, Bulk 1, Hands 2, Range 60 ft. Traits: Deadly d10, Propulsive. A compact bow favoured by scouts and mobile archers.
Dagger: 1d4 piercing, Bulk L, Hands 1, Range 10 ft (thrown). Traits: Agile, Finesse, Thrown 10 ft, Versatile S. A light and versatile blade useful both in melee and as a thrown weapon.
Greataxe: 1d12 slashing, Bulk 2, Hands 2. Traits: Sweep, Forceful. A heavy two-handed axe that cleaves through enemies with tremendous force.
Rapier: 1d6 piercing, Bulk 1, Hands 1. Traits: Deadly d8, Disarm, Finesse. A slender thrusting blade prized for its precision and reach.
Warhammer: 1d8 bludgeoning, Bulk 1, Hands 1. Traits: Shove. A heavy hammer used in battle, capable of knocking enemies off balance.
Crossbow: 1d8 piercing, Bulk 1, Hands 2, Range 120 ft. Traits: Reload 1. A mechanical ranged weapon with excellent range, requiring one action to reload between shots.
Staff: 1d4 bludgeoning, Bulk 1, Hands 1 or 2. Traits: Two-Hand d8. A wooden walking staff that can be wielded as a simple melee weapon.
Shortspear: 1d6 piercing, Bulk 1, Hands 1, Range 20 ft (thrown). Traits: Agile, Thrown 20 ft. A compact spear that can be thrown short distances or used in close combat.
Hand Crossbow: 1d6 piercing, Bulk L, Hands 1, Range 60 ft. Traits: Reload 1. A compact one-handed crossbow favoured by rogues and duelists.
